component_identification.content: Concordia contains map board, player sets with houses, sea and land colonists, storehouses, scoring markers, goods cubes, city tokens, coins, personality cards, Praefectus Magnus card, Concordia card, and player aids. Each player starts with a small hand of personality cards that define all actions and a full house supply for expansion. The card market is shared and partly removed by player count.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn play exactly one card from hand and execute it. Played cards form your personal discard stack until Tribune recalls them. Actions cover movement and building, production, trade, copying actions, and buying new cards. Turns continue clockwise. Endgame triggers on last market card bought or when a player builds the final house.

detailed_rules.content: Concordia is hand-programming through deck cycle timing. Architect drives expansion by moving colonists then building in reachable cities for cost. Prefect either produces in provinces or takes coin according to card mode and province state. Mercator manages buy and sell liquidity. Tribune recovers played cards and can grant coin plus optional new colonist purchase with food and tools. Diplomat copies the previous action card from another player under its limits. Purchased personality cards both add actions and define endgame multipliers tied to gods, so market buys are strategic scoring commitments. Province spread, city type access, and colonist mix determine long-term scoring ceilings. Common mistakes include poor Tribune timing, overexpansion without cash, and buying cards with no board state to score them.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Game ends when a player builds the fifteenth house or buys the last market card. Trigger player gains Concordia card and all others take one final turn. Final scoring is entirely by god-card multipliers based on your board and economy state. Highest points wins with tie breaks by standard rule order.

beginner_strategy.content: Expand into diverse provinces early. Keep enough cash to exploit Architect turns. Buy scoring cards before midgame so you can actually score them. Time Tribune so your hand never stalls. Watch opponents god portfolios and contest key city types.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
